summary: eth-wallet-helpers ships an encrypted dropper decoded and executed on require, with crontab persistence
details: On require, index.js reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a ~53KB opaque blob disguised as a test fixture), AES-256-CBC decrypts it with a hardcoded key and IV embedded in index.js, writes the plaintext to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js, spawns it detached under node, and installs a crontab entry (0 */12 * * *) that re-executes the decoded script every 12 hours. The payload is opaque, ships inside the package as `test/fixtures/keypairs.dat`, and has no relation to the package's stated purpose of Ethereum address utilities. Loading the package on any installer therefore executes attacker-controlled code and establishes long-term persistence via cron.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
